introSection: Shymkent Gorki was a steel roller coaster previously located at Ken Baba Ethnic Theme Park in Shymkent, South Kazakhstan, Kazakhstan.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The coaster used to operate on the Italian fair circuit with an unknown showman. It was later bought by Furuvik, where it opened as Superloopen. It eventually closed in 2000, and was sold to Schwarzkopf who in turn sold it to Adlerkurort Metro Park. Its full opening year here was unknown, but it would close in 2018, being relocated to Ken Baba Ethnic Theme Park. It eventually closed in 2021, being dismantled. Its current whereabouts are unknown.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Red track and unpainted supports. The loop is painted yellow, with it being white at Furuvik.

fullText:
Shymkent Gorki was a steel roller coaster previously located at Ken Baba Ethnic Theme Park in Shymkent, South Kazakhstan, Kazakhstan.

The coaster used to operate on the Italian fair circuit with an unknown showman. It was later bought by Furuvik, where it opened as Superloopen. It eventually closed in 2000, and was sold to Schwarzkopf who in turn sold it to Adlerkurort Metro Park. Its full opening year here was unknown, but it would close in 2018, being relocated to Ken Baba Ethnic Theme Park. It eventually closed in 2021, being dismantled. Its current whereabouts are unknown.



Red track and unpainted supports. The loop is painted yellow, with it being white at Furuvik.
